message:
feat(policy): add PolicySurface dimension for subsystem-level rule matching

Introduce PolicySurface enum to the policy engine, adding a "which
subsystem is this request coming from?" dimension to policy decisions.
This lets policy rules discriminate not just by action and actor, but
by the originating subsystem surface.

PolicySurface variants: Unknown, Mux, Swarm, Robot, Connector,
Workflow, Mcp, Ipc.

Key changes:
- policy.rs: new PolicySurface enum with as_str(), default_for_actor(),
  Display impl; surface field added to DecisionContext and PolicyInput
  with serde default for backwards compat; PolicyInput::new()
  auto-populates surface from actor kind; with_surface() builder;
  matches_rule() checks surfaces field with case-insensitive matching;
  PolicyGatedInjector and pane_eval_send marked as Mux surface
- config.rs: add surfaces: Vec<String> to PolicyRuleMatch, integrate
  into specificity() (+1 when non-empty) and is_catch_all()
- mcp_tools.rs: annotate tool handlers with surfaces — WaGetText/Search/
  Send as Mux, WaWorkflowRun as Workflow, WaReserve/Release as Swarm
- proptest_policy.rs: add surface: Unknown to arb_decision_context()

7 new tests covering surface matching, trait derives, serde roundtrip,
actor-to-surface default mapping, and injector surface annotation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -181,6 +181,7 @@ fn arb_decision_context() -> impl Strategy<Value = DecisionContext> {
                     timestamp_ms,
                     action,
                     actor,
+                    surface: PolicySurface::Unknown,
                     pane_id,
                     domain,
                     capabilities,
